purpose: S1 is the machine-readable geometry source layer. Its role is not to fully implement geometry, but to identify what geometry it is in a normalized form so that P-layer programs can later compile from it.

design_rule.core_definition: S1 stores normalized geometry source definitions for future P-layer implementation.

field_notes.geom_id: Cross-layer stable geometry identity key shared with L/P/M.
field_notes.source_layer: Must be S1 for this schema.
field_notes.name: Human-readable geometry name.
field_notes.subtype: Specific normalized subtype such as minkowski, lorentzian, projective_hilbert, symplectic, etc.
field_notes.core_objects: Primary mathematical objects relevant to this geometry.
field_notes.core_relations: Primary relations/invariants/structures relevant to this geometry.
field_notes.required_operations: Operations needed so P-layer can eventually compile or manipulate this geometry.
field_notes.source_refs: References or provenance pointers for where this geometry definition comes from.
